name: Waimanalo
desc: Rural beach town — gorgeous long beach, tight local community, but known for rougher edges and tent camps.
insider: Waimanalo Beach has been rated the #1 beach in America by Dr. Beach — the turquoise water against the Ko'olau backdrop is unreal
Keneke's is the local plate lunch institution — get the garlic shrimp or kalbi ribs and eat at the picnic tables
Be respectful of the community — this is Hawaiian homestead land with deep cultural roots and generational families
The Hawaii Polo Club hosts free Sunday matches in season at the Waimanalo polo grounds — bring a blanket and a cooler
Waimanalo Country Farms has a seasonal corn maze and pumpkin patch — the best family fall activity on the windward side
Sweet Home Waimanalo serves elevated comfort food with local ingredients — the fried chicken is incredible
Car break-ins happen at beach parking lots — never leave valuables visible, not even a phone charger
The Sea Life Park area marks the start of Waimanalo — it's a commercial tourist attraction but the views driving past are free
Bellows Air Force Station beach opens to the public on weekends — it's one of the most beautiful camping beaches on Oahu
Sherwood Forest (Waimanalo Bay Beach Park) is the local name for the main beach — the ironwood tree canopy is perfect for shade
